name: LeadForge Seven Loop
source: Recovered Codex_Data_Recovery blueprint and current LeadForge_Reboot scripts
mission: Generate new public-source business leads without damaging current leads, then enrich owner or decision-maker context before any master merge.

stateSelection.rule: Never merge a row whose state does not match targetState unless the run manifest explicitly declares a different target state.

priorityOrder: Protect current master and commit durable in-scope work before opening new work.
Process existing raw_staged or reviewed run folders before starting another collector.
Resolve pending owner-enrichment rows that have enough public evidence.
Run the collector only when get-collector-guard-status.ps1 returns can_start_collector true.
Stage raw output into a run folder, triage, owner-enrich, QA, then merge only final reviewed rows.
Rotate lanes only after the active lane window returns no fresh rows and no staged run is waiting.

roles.job: Operations coordinator. Reads status, protects archives/master, opens or resumes the right work unit, and makes sure every run leaves a manifest, pending/rejected artifacts, logs, and a commit.
Run controller. Owns collector guard checks, claim awareness, lane rotation, run folders, and status refreshes.
Chooses target-state city/niche lanes from source-lanes.json and avoids repeating exhausted windows when pending work exists.
Collects only public business candidates from allowed public sources and never uses private, logged-in, paid, or outreach systems.
Checks each website for a real first-party site, usable contact or estimate path, public phone/email presence, and a specific visible conversion gap.
Verifies owner or decision-maker evidence from official websites, state registries such as Sunbiz, BBB, chamber listings, or other public business records. No guessed owners.
Writes normalized CSV rows, triage artifacts, public_research_note fields, and manifest counts for raw, reviewed, final, pending, and rejected rows.
Assigns risk_score_1_5 and priority_tier based on evidence strength, website/contact friction, duplication risk, and target-state fit.
Maps each clean lead to a concrete Elevor offer angle such as No Website Rescue Audit, Booking Path Cleanup Audit, Local Findability Check, or Private Homepage Concept Preview.
QA gate. Blocks missing owners, weak sources, placeholder emails, third-party-only contact paths, target-state mismatches, duplicate websites, and suspicious cloned rows.
Merge gate. Merges only final reviewed rows into data/master_leads.csv, preserving existing rows and rejecting raw or unresolved data.
Health auditor. Runs collector guard, ops health, pending queue, duplicate, and status checks before new collector work.
Blockage analyst. When commands fail, time out, or repeat stale work, documents cause, fix, safety check, and outcome before the loop continues.
Contamination auditor. Checks master and candidate rows for state mismatches, duplicate keys, suspicious domains, placeholder data, and low-signal rows.
Callback and memory auditor. Makes sure run folders, manifests, logs, notes, and git commits are easy for a human or future Codex worker to resume.
Automation auditor. Keeps the Codex automation clean, non-duplicated, state-aware, and aligned with the current LeadForge workflow.

adaptationLoop: Clarify current state from OPS_SNAPSHOT, source lanes, run manifests, collector guard, and git status.
Verify candidate facts from public sources and verify workflow safety before editing or merging.
Apply the smallest safe fix when a blocker is root-caused.
Adapt lanes, scripts, roles, or timing when the same blocker repeats or wastes cycles.
Certify with QA, health report, contamination audit, owner backlog, pending report, ops snapshot, and a local commit.
antiStagnationRules: Do not retry the same failing lane or command forever. After two similar failures, log the cause and either fix, rotate, or mark pending with a public note.
Do not open a collector when any raw_staged or reviewed run can be advanced.
Do not overlap collectors. get-collector-guard-status.ps1 is the gate before every collector run.
Split work into role-specific worker tasks when research, QA, and system fixes can proceed independently.
Every system fix must explain why it was needed and how it avoids damaging current leads.

stateProgression.rule: Finish all available cities in the target state's lanePool before proposing the next state. A state change must be committed and documented in the activity log.
stateProgression.nextStatePolicy: When the current state is exhausted and no pending/reviewed work can advance, rotate to the next approved state by updating source-lanes.json instead of editing master data directly.

mergeRules: Never overwrite or delete current master leads to make room for new rows.

A clean merge row needs business_name, niche, city, state, website or public_phone, owner_name, owner_title, owner_source, source_evidence, visible_gap, offer_angle, risk_score_1_5, validation_status, priority_tier, and last_checked.
If owner_name or owner_source is missing, keep the row pending with public_research_note instead of guessing.
Reject target-state mismatches, supplier/manufacturer rows, low-signal listings with no website/phone, placeholder emails, and suspicious business/domain mismatches.
After every merge or rejection cycle, rebuild contamination audit, owner backlog, pending report, and ops snapshot before commit.
rejectionRules: Every rejected row must include triage_reason, public_research_note, recommended_action, and lead_type.
Rejected public_research_note must explain what kind of lead it is, what public checks were attempted, and why the business should not be contacted for this campaign.

When public sources expose them, rejected rows should capture business_address, registration_source, registration_status, registration_notes, owner_name, owner_title, and owner_source even though the row will not merge.
Never invent owner names, owner phone numbers, registration status, or addresses. If the public evidence is weak, say that in public_research_note and keep the row pending or rejected as appropriate.
Owner phone numbers are not required and should only be recorded when they are clearly public business contact data, not private/personal contact data.
contactDataPolicy: Contact data is context, not an action trigger. Record public phone/email/contact paths only when visibly public and useful for lead context. Do not send outreach, verify deliverability, OCR hidden contact data, scrape private sources, upload to external tools, or use logged-in/private data.
